evidence: Router handlers in src/cowabunga_api/routers/openai/ (threads.py, messages.py, embeddings.py, chat.py) show direct, readable control flow with early validation and explicit CRUD calls
CRUD base pattern in src/cowabunga_api/data/crud_base.py is consistent across specialized CRUD classes, providing balanced extraction
Backend helpers in src/cowabunga_api/backend/helpers.py are focused and single-purpose (recv_completion, recv_chat, grpc_chat_role, read_chunks)
src/cowabunga_sdk/llm.py LLM decorator is a 215-line monolith defining an inline class with 7 nested methods, obscuring control flow
src/cowabunga_evals/runners/niah_runner.py run_experiment is ~140 lines mixing file I/O, API orchestration, scoring, and in-place data mutation in a single method
src/cowabunga_api/backend/composer.py generate_message_for_thread and stream_generate_message_for_thread contain identical 16-line tool-resources lookup blocks (copy-paste duplication)
src/cowabunga_api/data/database/turso_auth.py _authenticate_api_key, _authenticate_jwt, and _authenticate_session each contain ~15 lines of nearly identical SQL and User object construction
src/cowabunga_api/data/database/turso_client.py TursoAuthClient.get_user defines UserResponse and User dataclasses inside the method body, recreating them on every call

src/cowabunga_api/typedef/assistants/assistant_types.py request_checks_and_modifications defines two async helper functions (new_vector_store_from_file_ids, attach_existing_vector_store_from_id) inside the method rather than extracting them

low_level_elegance.dimension_character: The codebase shows a split personality at the function level: router handlers and CRUD operations are generally direct and well-extracted, but several core modules contain significant monoliths (LLM decorator, NIAH runner, composer duplication) and occasional micro-fragmentation (inline dataclasses, nested functions). Helper extraction style is consistent within layers but varies across them.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: The score reflects solid craftsmanship in the API surface layer offset by deep structural problems in core modules. The LLM decorator monolith and duplicated composer logic are the primary drags. A score of 70.0 places this above 'needs attention' but below 'strong' because the defects are localized yet deep.

issues.summary: LLM decorator is a 215-line monolith with inline class and nested closures
evidence: The LLM() decorator function spans lines 50-265 and defines an entire NewClass inline with 7 async methods
create_chat_completion_response and create_completion_response are 25-line nested functions that differ only in response type, yet are not parameterized
ChatComplete, ChatCompleteStream, Complete, and CompleteStream share nearly identical finish-reason and token-counting logic that is duplicated 4 times
issues.suggestion: Extract NewClass to module level, parameterize the response-factory helpers, and extract shared finish-reason/token-counting logic into a single helper method.

issues.summary: run_experiment is a ~140-line monolith mixing I/O, API calls, scoring, and cleanup
evidence: run_experiment contains a tight loop over self.niah_data that writes temp files, uploads via API, creates assistants, creates threads, runs evals, scores results, and mutates instance state (self.current_file, self.current_assistant)
In-place dataset mutation via self.niah_data.add_column() is scattered at the end of the method after the loop
The method body interleaves file-system I/O, network I/O, scoring arithmetic, and logging without clear phase boundaries
issues.suggestion: Extract per-row evaluation into a _evaluate_single_row() method, separate setup/teardown phases from the scoring loop, and move dataset augmentation out of the experiment method.

issues.summary: Identical 16-line tool-resources lookup duplicated in sync and stream composer methods
evidence: generate_message_for_thread lines 179-195 and stream_generate_message_for_thread lines 249-264 contain verbatim identical CRUDAssistant lookup and tool_resources resolution logic
Both branches perform the same fallback chain: check passed tool_resources -> query assistant -> validate type -> model_validate dump -> else None
issues.suggestion: Extract a private _resolve_tool_resources(request, session, tool_resources) method that both sync and stream variants can call.

issues.summary: User-construction SQL and object building is duplicated across three authentication methods
evidence: _authenticate_api_key (lines 132-151), _authenticate_jwt (lines 163-182), and _authenticate_session (lines 211-230) each contain nearly identical SELECT * FROM users queries and User(...) constructor calls
The only varying parts are the token-lookup queries; user fetching and validation are identical
issues.suggestion: Extract a _fetch_active_user(db, user_id) -> User helper that centralizes the user query, existence check, and User construction.

issues.summary: Dataclass types defined inside a method body, recreated on every call
evidence: TursoAuthClient.get_user defines UserResponse and User dataclasses at lines 234-243 inside the async method body
These types are recreated at runtime on every get_user() call and are not local to the method's control flow
issues.suggestion: Move UserResponse and User dataclass definitions to module level alongside the existing TursoResult dataclass.

issues.summary: Async helper functions defined inside a method rather than extracted
evidence: request_checks_and_modifications (lines 88-201) defines new_vector_store_from_file_ids and attach_existing_vector_store_from_id as nested async functions inside the method body
The method is 113 lines long; extracting the two helpers would reduce it to ~40 lines of orchestration logic
issues.suggestion: Extract new_vector_store_from_file_ids and attach_existing_vector_store_from_id to private methods on the class or module-level helpers.

root_causes: Core framework modules (sdk, evals) were built under time pressure and prioritized 'works' over extraction balance
Decorator pattern in llm.py was used as a one-size-fits-all extension mechanism without considering readability cost

add.header: Router handlers show direct, readable control flow
add.description: OpenAI-compliant routers (threads, messages, embeddings, chat) consistently use early validation, explicit CRUD calls, and clear error boundaries. This pattern is a positive anchor for the codebase.

add.header: CRUD base extraction is balanced and consistent
add.description: The Generic CRUDBase in data/crud_base.py provides a clean, bounded abstraction that all specialized CRUD classes inherit uniformly. Extraction level is appropriate for the layer.

add.header: Backend helpers are focused and single-purpose
add.description: backend/helpers.py contains well-scoped utilities (recv_completion, recv_chat, grpc_chat_role, read_chunks) each doing one thing without side effects.

add.header: Core modules contain monoliths and micro-fragmentation
add.description: SDK decorator (llm.py), evals runner (niah_runner.py), and database client (turso_client.py) suffer from either monolithic inline classes or dataclasses defined inside methods. This inconsistency drags down the overall low-level elegance.
